title: The Morality of Instrumentality
wikitext:
[]: The Morality of Instrumentality             Quote Post by NemZ » Tue Sep 03, 2013 8:34 am [Split from "The Moral of Rebuild" . - Monk] Bagheera wrote: This is silly. I accept it as bad because efforts to make it happen thus far have wrecked the world and killed millions of people.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Tue Sep 03, 2013 5:35 pm NemZ wrote: And yes Bags, I have no trouble separating Instrumentality as a state of being from the misdeeds that are carried out to achieve it. There might be a better way that just takes longer, or alternatives that don't let Seele have quite the scenario they want. We also don't know if there was the same 'must do this or angels inevitably will kill us all' thing, as we haven't really gotten the rebuild take on ep21 yet (and we quite likely may never know, now that Seele is gone).

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Tue Sep 03, 2013 6:01 pm ...and it actually might cover that, so who knows? Are we even sure that 3I actually killed anyone, or did it just turn them into the mass of 01 bodies just waiting for the signal to wake back up?

[]:             Quote Post by one-eyed » Tue Sep 03, 2013 7:27 pm I met people who thought the idea of an Instrumentality enticing. To live forever in a fantasy world where all your desires are satisfied, but frankly, I'm sorry, to me this should be the ultimate escape from reality, the dream of opium and crack addicts and like. Does not attract me, because I like my life in spite of the problems; however I can imagine someone like Gendo, who despite having a blessed life and have had only one bad luck in life to try such a thing for not knowing how to deal with reality. In general, when someone wants to be God is because he is not strong enough to be a man and how the Church teaches: trying become God you end up becoming the Devil.

[]:             Quote Post by Stillborn » Tue Sep 03, 2013 7:42 pm Church teaches also how worthless and horrible human is, and that without the eternal big brother who tells us how to act, we would kill and rape each other. I agree with the "horrible" part. However the problem with our concept of escaping reality, is that reality will eventually catch up to you. Instrumentality don't have this problem. Once everyone is fused, their dreams BECOME reality. There is no "real life" to suddenly make them wake up as fat otaku in mother's cellar. Instrumentality is evolution. Another form. What's so bad in trying to be improved? If it wasn't for defiance agains natural order, we wouldn't even create medicine for pneumosis. So what's so wrong in trying to reunite with loved person if it IS possible apparently? I don't condemn Gendo or Seele's goal. Just their forcefull and callous methods.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Tue Sep 03, 2013 8:01 pm yep, pretty much what Stillborn said... Once instrumentality starts there it stops being an escape and becomes the new reality to navigate. Facing the hardships of real life is only a virtue because there's no viable alternative. As soon as you introduce such an option sticking with the old one is a stubborn choice, like using a landline rather than a smartphone. It's much the same as faith... if religious people had any real evidence they wouldn't need to fall back on such a dubious virtue to justify their beliefs to themselves.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Tue Sep 03, 2013 8:11 pm That's great until you read the fine print and understand what Instrumentality really is. If we were talking Eclipse Phase it'd be great, but we aren't -- death of self is part and parcel of the concept. So, mindless cell in a mindless organism that just sits there forever? You consider being an immortal carrot an upgrade? I don't. What good is losing pain if you lose everything else in the process?

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Tue Sep 03, 2013 8:14 pm ^ I guess that's why there are people fighting against Human Instrumentality. Misato in both NGE and NTE does this to some extent. (A greater extent in NTE. Heck, she's got her own friggin' rag-tag navy backing her up on this quest.)

[]:             Quote Post by Stillborn » Tue Sep 03, 2013 8:21 pm Assuming you WILL become mindless carrot/cell. And Assuming it would bother you. There is the theory that if you make a specific scan of planet Earth it shows great resemblanse to human brain, and humans are neurons. And do you feel like a neuron? Before humans were afraid of many things. Until they learned about them. As i said, Seele made mistake of forcing it. That scared people, because we are instinctually afraid of the unknown.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Tue Sep 03, 2013 8:52 pm Stillborn wrote: Assuming you WILL become mindless carrot/cell.

[]:             Quote Post by Stillborn » Tue Sep 03, 2013 9:10 pm You will die anyway. Why not accep it as you say we should accept death? It's not much different.

[]:             Quote Post by Guy Nacks » Tue Sep 03, 2013 9:14 pm Best and possibly most accurate representation of death in film? The final scene of the Sopranos.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Tue Sep 03, 2013 9:34 pm Stillborn wrote: You will die anyway. Why not accep it as you say we should accept death? It's not much different.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Tue Sep 03, 2013 9:38 pm I think you're being overly simplistic in equating instrumentality to death. EoTV certainly didn't show it that way, and there is of course the various 'starchild' sort of theories of what might have happened after EoE if it had gone to completion.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Tue Sep 03, 2013 9:49 pm NemZ wrote: I think you're being overly simplistic in equating instrumentality to death. EoTV certainly didn't show it that way, and there is of course the various 'starchild' sort of theories of what might have happened after EoE if it had gone to completion.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Tue Sep 03, 2013 10:03 pm If we have good reason to be afraid of the death of self, why is it the endpoint of so many spiritual paths?

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Tue Sep 03, 2013 10:05 pm It's very much on-topic. The topic changed when you weren't looking. And the worms don't still retain your memories, desires, and perspective in general.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Tue Sep 03, 2013 10:30 pm Chuckman wrote: If we have good reason to be afraid of the death of self, why is it the endpoint of so many spiritual paths?

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Tue Sep 03, 2013 10:56 pm Eva is rather schizophrenic on that point. On the one hand, it's very clear in its message that religion is just another form of escape from the hedgehog's dilemma. (Yui is a criticism of the same urge in mechanistic materialists- she's so obsessed with the eternal, if only in the form of turning herself into a kind of grave marker, that she discards the here and now and abandons her son and husband who love her, in favor of an utterly insane goal) On the other hand, it takes place in a setting where magic is literally real and is studied as a scientific field and there is technology based on it. People in NGE actually have souls in a provable sense. Luminous beings are we, not this crude matter. that can transcend the body and join together in a gestalt being. It changes the equation a little. Also, we can't really be sure that Instrumentality in Rebuild is the same thing, that Gendo and Seele have the same goals, or that it works the same way. I think it's clear at this point that assuming any metaphysical details from NGE apply to the movie series without explicit proof is a skewed approach, given how much has clearly been changed.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Tue Sep 03, 2013 11:17 pm I like what has been said so far enough that if I have to move the topic over into (NGE) Discussion I will, if that will keep it going. That's probably where it has to go anyway because we know almost nothing about Rebuild Instrumentality and what little we do know seems different already anyway. For now, don't anybody worry about whether we're talking about NGE or NTE Instrumentality here. Think of the topic as covering both. EDIT: Looks like Tines went ahead and moved it.
summary:

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Tue Sep 03, 2013 5:35 pm NemZ wrote: And yes Bags,
